title: Problems with the English dub
wikitext:
[]: Problems with the English dub...             Quote Post by FrDougal9000 » Mon May 13, 2013 11:54 am Okay, it's pretty much guaranteed that the English dub for Evangelion is, and will most likely remain, the most divisive dub in the universe; for every person that loves it, there's someone who can't stand the hell out of it. However, I think we can agree that regardless of our actual opinion on the dub, it had a heck of a lot of issues/flaws/problems etc. So, I thought it'd be fun to try compile all the flaws of the dub to see what we can come up with. (NOTE: This only refers to the issues seen in ADV's dub for the TV series and Manga's dub for Death and ReBirth+EoE; this isn't about FUNimation's dub for Rebuild, though if you found flaws with that, you can include those as well) I'll start the list off and you guys can add anything I missed out on and after some time, I'll compile a big giant list together. And now, without further ado...LET'S BEGIN! TV Series Dub Problems: -Making Kensuke sound like a white version of Urkel -Screwing up Asuka's 'No, this isn't me!' scenes in the Director's Cut (It was supposed to be several different actresses as Asuka, not just Tiffany Grant over and over again) -Recording dialogue two episodes at a time (This led to misjudging characters and making some sound weird compared to later episodes ie Misato) -Having about 3 billion different actors for the SIDE CHARACTERS!!! -Mistranslating the subtitles for the OP theme to make it more singable (which didn't even work!) -Maya's original VA was terrible (no offence but the voice was just really...dull) Movie Dub Problems: -Replacing the slap with the gunshot in Death and ReBirth (It doesn't sound like a gunshot!) -Adding in the jam sound when the JSSDF mook gets killed and during Instrumentality in EoE -Giving us the wonders of Black Hyuga and Bored Fuyutsuki -Mistranslating the nature of Lillith and the Lilim (Like things weren't confusing enough!) Well, that's all I've got for now. Maybe, I'll come up with some more but you guys can take it from here. All I have to say here is simply have fun!

[]:             Quote Post by Catamari » Mon May 13, 2013 11:58 am This one isn't so much a problem, as it is just an inconsistency, but I feel it is worth mentioning. Touji's voice in the dub tends to drift in and out of accent. In the original Japanese, he had an Osakan accent (it was even heavier in the manga), but in the dub, his accents tend to drift in and out, nearer the beginning of the show, he has more of a southern accent, but in the late episodes (particularly 25/26), Touji has a more mid-western accent. I'm not sure if that was intentional, it's just something that bugs me. Irrelevant and Spammy  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon May 13, 2013 12:12 pm This is not a sub vs dub thread. Posts like Catamari's post above will be treated as spam. The purpose of this thread is to point out problems with the dub, translation errors, alteration, etc., not whether you prefer one over the other.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon May 13, 2013 12:25 pm I do remember ADV ging back and fixing some of the translation problems in the dub for the Platinum Collection. I think Allison Ship-Keith (Misato's dubber) says "mental contamination" in Ep. 2 rather than "mental injury" or whatever.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon May 13, 2013 9:49 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I think Allison Ship-Keith (Misato's dubber) says "mental contamination" in Ep. 2 rather than "mental injury" or whatever.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon May 13, 2013 10:43 pm Fuytsuki's boredom would be logical if his dialogue were rewritten to reflect his inner thoughts. "Is this over yet? I've been waiting twelve years for a glimpse of the woman that friendzoned me before I explode. God damn it why is she wearing a shirt what is this bullshi-SPLORTCH .

[]:             Quote Post by EVAfacepalm » Tue May 14, 2013 12:07 am Not to mention the early episodes' dubs all have really stilted dialogue. It's fine by episode 5 or so, but before then you have some really unconvincing reads that sound either too dramatic for the situation or not intense enough.

[]: Re: Problems with the English dub...             Quote Post by user-02 » Tue May 14, 2013 8:32 am FrDougal9000 wrote: Okay, it's pretty much guaranteed that the English dub for Evangelion is, and will most likely remain, the most divisive dub in the universe; for every person that loves it, there's someone who can't stand the hell out of it. However, I think we can agree that regardless of our actual opinion on the dub, it had a heck of a lot of issues/flaws/problems etc. So, I thought it'd be fun to try compile all the flaws of the dub to see what we can come up with.

[]: Re: Problems with the English dub...             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue May 14, 2013 9:11 am user-02 wrote: Opinion, although I'll grant you that Kensuke's American voice is a divisive one (as is the character of Kensuke in general). I happen to think it's a little over the top, but that it fits him. In fact, I find it one of the more interesting acting choices in the dub, believe it or not.

[]:             Quote Post by Ringworm128 » Thu May 16, 2013 11:36 pm "Sweet Jesus it's an anti-AT field" is my only real problem with the dub.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Oct 17, 2015 10:57 pm I know, I know, thread time. Noticing somebody's current sig made me curious enough to investigate, so here I am. So uh, were these lyrics ADV's OP dubtitles from back in the day? (Something I only know vaguely about.) They work as a good attempt for the OP, but miss a lot of what's actually being said, due to the nature of dubtitles and all... Lyrics in case the link is Internet-MIA someday wrote: Like an angel who has forsaken sympathy Rise up, young boy, and make yourself a legend! Blue skies and beckoning winds Are trying to open the door of your heart But you’re gazing at me as if you cannot see You just stand there smiling vacantly Your eyes hold such innocence You’re blind to the fate that will soon come to pass But it’s summoning you with a touch like a sigh You seek it without knowing why But someday I know that you will be awakened And your eyes will finally see These wings on your back are just waiting to guide you– The future will soon set you free! This cruel thesis, a spiteful angel’s litany, Will soon soar from the window of your destiny White hot pathos, a desecrated symphony Stay true to this and don’t betray your memory! Come now, reach out—you must embrace reality Rise up, young boy, and make yourself a legend! You’ve slept for ever so long Embraced in affection and cradled with care But with morning’s approach comes an echoing cry It tells you to wave your dreams goodbye And the moon is shining above It wishes to show just how fragile you are You’re so frail and slight in its silvery light; Our fate is not one we can fight But someday I hope that we’ll be reunited If that is what’s destined to be Perhaps we’ll discover that elusive bible And then we will finally be free! This cruel thesis, a spiteful angel’s mockery, Will bring with it the sorrow of futility Hold on tightly and do not lose vitality You must wake up, for now the dream is history! Come now, reach out—you must embrace humanity Rise up, young boy, and make yourself a legend! Love is the thread of the story that’s woven Of future and present and past I may not ever ascend to the heavens But I know my love will still last This cruel thesis, a spiteful angel’s litany, Will soon soar from the window of your destiny White hot pathos, a desecrated symphony Stay true to this and don’t betray your memory! Come now, reach out—you must embrace reality Rise up, young boy, and make yourself a legend!

[]:             Quote Post by megahobbit » Tue Oct 20, 2015 5:42 pm Controversial opinion besides some obvious flubs and miscasts I don't mind the dialogue changes and such between the dub and the original. I find that especially with End of Eva it the dub treats the characters much differently. EOE dub is way more sympathetic to Shinji and cruel to Asuka. Its an interesting reinterpretation.

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Wed Oct 21, 2015 4:49 am One thing that I don't like about the English dub is that it is where many people's hate comes from, Spike Spencer as Shinji and his grating voice, and a few misconceptions like Rei is an emotionless doll, AWL's voice performance. Though while I actually liked AWL's voice as Rei, it is inaccurate in terms of how the character is meant to be potrayed.

[]:             Quote Post by Iuvenal » Wed Oct 21, 2015 8:01 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: I know, I know, thread time. Noticing somebody's current sig made me curious enough to investigate, so here I am. So uh, were these lyrics ADV's OP dubtitles from back in the day? (Something I only know vaguely about.) They work as a good attempt for the OP, but miss a lot of what's actually being said, due to the nature of dubtitles and all...

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Wed Oct 21, 2015 9:45 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: I know, I know, thread time. Noticing somebody's current sig made me curious enough to investigate, so here I am. So uh, were these lyrics ADV's OP dubtitles from back in the day? (Something I only know vaguely about.) They work as a good attempt for the OP, but miss a lot of what's actually being said, due to the nature of dubtitles and all...

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Wed Oct 21, 2015 5:45 pm In general I like the dub and associate the characters with those voices due to familiarity, but there are truly some unintentionally hilarious, cringeworthy lines and line deliveries scattered throughout. Most of them are in the first few episodes, I think, and then it seems to level off. But then just when you think you're safe, you hit episode 21 and you get the masterwork that is "I'm dating Gendo Rokubungi... a lot! "

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Thu Oct 29, 2015 8:18 pm You have probably read these points multiple times before but anyways: Honestly, the Eva dub is like someone took the Genji Monogatari and it was "translated" and '''''''localized''''' by the same people who did the Romeo and Juliet emojis book. It's not so much the quality itself -which it certainly not even near the original- as it is that it kills a lot of the nuances and layers the original voices had. I can't even listen to Shinji's U.S. VA more than 10 seconds. Where Megumi Ogata's performance is vibrant and brimming with so much depth and emotions, everything Spike says is tinted in that garish ultra-nasal... i don't even know what to call it. I don't know, maybe it's that U.S. voice acting doesn't lend itself very well to a very Japanese work. Overall, i think the problems isn't so much in the higher order aspects of the production -how it was directed and acted, etc.- but the more essential/mundane basics of how it was acted itself. As an enthusiast in art, writing, and singing/spoken voice, it frankly came off to me like it just wasn't very good voice-acting.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Oct 29, 2015 10:06 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: So uh, were these lyrics ADV's OP dubtitles from back in the day?

[]: Re: Problems with the English dub...             Quote Post by lily liver » Mon Mar 07, 2016 11:56 pm FrDougal9000 wrote: Adding in the jam sound when the JSSDF mook gets killed and during Instrumentality in EoE

[]: Re: Problems with the English dub...             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Mon Mar 07, 2016 11:59 pm ^ That and who ever voiced acted the bridge bunnies, like com'on now seriously?!
